codeReview.summary: The feature requirements are met: both edge proxy paths were moved to ReverseProxy Rewrite handlers, client-supplied forwarding headers are sanitized before backend forwarding, and dedicated security tests cover spoofed XFF/X-Real-IP, multi-hop replacement, and cross-feature bootstrap/rate-limit behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture shared-state doc is now stale about forwarding behavior and could mislead future workers on XFF trust assumptions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:7 still says proxyControlPlane preserves inbound X-Forwarded-For when present, but commit 5ec8c36 switched both proxy paths to Rewrite-based sanitization and replacement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The security-backend-worker skill does not currently document the ReverseProxy Director vs Rewrite forwarding-header behavior, which was central to implementing this feature correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff 2026-03-29T16-00-33-332Z__xff-sanitization__025d86a3-6190-4b25-9930-6ab512013b06.json:100-101 suggests adding this pitfall to the skill; worker transcript shows investigation of duplicate XFF append behavior before landing the Rewrite approach.

summary: Review passed. Commit 5ec8c36 correctly enforces edge-generated forwarding headers for both control-plane and app proxy paths and includes targeted security regression tests; no correctness/security blocking issues were identified in the feature diff.
